userStories.title: Confirm worker-boe idempotency and no double-ingestion with cron-boe-daily
acceptanceCriteria: Running worker-boe --run-once twice does not duplicate articulo rows
Understand whether worker-boe (continuous) and cron-boe-daily are running the same logic
If they duplicate work, document the intended split (continuous=stream, cron=backfill?) in AGENTS.md
Row count in articulo is stable after second run
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose exec postgres psql -U esdata -d esdata -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM articulo'; docker compose run --rm worker-boe python boe.py --run-once; docker compose exec postgres psql -U esdata -d esdata -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM articulo'

userStories.title: Run seed-modelos.py — populate aeat_modelo with 25 official AEAT models
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/seed-modelos.py runs without errors inside the correct Docker context
aeat_modelo table contains >= 25 rows after seed
Rows have non-null codigo, nombre, tipo fields
userStories.verificationCommand: docker exec -w /app esdata-api-1 python seed-modelos.py --db-url 'postgresql://esdata:esdata_dev@postgres:5432/esdata' && docker exec esdata-api-1 python -c 'import psycopg; c=psycopg.connect("postgresql://esdata:esdata_dev@postgres:5432/esdata"); r=c.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM aeat_modelo").fetchone()[0]; print(r); c.close(); assert r >= 25' && echo PASS || echo FAIL

userStories.title: Run seed-modelos-v2.py — populate modelo_articulo relationships
acceptanceCriteria: Depends on S-02 passing first
scripts/seed-modelos-v2.py runs without errors
modelo_articulo table contains rows after seed
FK relationships to aeat_modelo are valid (zero orphaned rows)
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose run --rm app python scripts/seed-modelos-v2.py && docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modelo_articulo' | grep -vE '^0$' && echo PASS || echo FAIL

userStories.title: Resolve aeat_models.py Playwright dependency — enable Hacienda data ingestion
acceptanceCriteria: Determine definitively whether aeat_models.py can run via HTTP fallback or requires Playwright
If Playwright required: confirm Dockerfile.aeat builds successfully and worker-modelos can start
If HTTP fallback viable: test it and document which AEAT endpoints respond without JS
At minimum: worker-modelos starts without crash and logs a clear status (working or blocked-by-AEAT)
Decision and findings documented in AGENTS.md
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose run --rm worker-modelos python aeat_models.py --run-once 2>&1 | tail -20; echo EXIT:$?

userStories.title: Test worker-boe-modelos — populate BOE-sourced modelo data
acceptanceCriteria: worker-boe-modelos runs without errors
At least one of modelo_campana, modelo_casilla, modelo_normativa gets rows after run
Log output shows records processed > 0
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose run --rm worker-boe-modelos python boe_modelosWorker.py --run-once 2>&1 | tail -30; docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -c 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modelo_campana; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modelo_casilla;'

userStories.title: Test worker-dgt — verify DGT data ingestion
acceptanceCriteria: docker compose run --rm worker-dgt python dgt.py --run-once exits without unhandled exception
If it populates tables: row count > 0 in relevant DGT tables
If upstream is unavailable: worker logs a clear warning and exits cleanly (not crash)
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose run --rm worker-dgt python dgt.py --run-once 2>&1 | tail -20; echo EXIT:$?

userStories.title: Test worker-teac, worker-bdns, worker-borme — batch smoke test
acceptanceCriteria: Each worker runs --run-once without unhandled exception
Each worker exits with code 0 or logs a clear upstream-unavailable message
No worker silently swallows errors (no bare except: pass)
userStories.verificationCommand: for w in worker-teac worker-bdns worker-borme; do echo "=== $w ==="; docker compose run --rm $w --run-once 2>&1 | tail -10; done

userStories.title: Test worker-cnmv, worker-sepblac, worker-cendoj — batch smoke test
acceptanceCriteria: Same criteria as S-07 for these three workers
userStories.verificationCommand: for w in worker-cnmv worker-sepblac worker-cendoj; do echo "=== $w ==="; docker compose run --rm $w --run-once 2>&1 | tail -10; done

userStories.title: Test worker-eurlex, worker-bde, worker-cdi, worker-aepd — batch smoke test
acceptanceCriteria: Same criteria as S-07 for these four workers
userStories.verificationCommand: for w in worker-eurlex worker-bde worker-cdi worker-aepd; do echo "=== $w ==="; docker compose run --rm $w --run-once 2>&1 | tail -10; done

userStories.title: Fix cron-boe-modelos-daily missing systemd timer
acceptanceCriteria: esdata-boe-modelos-daily.timer and esdata-boe-modelos-daily.service created
Timer schedule matches docker-compose cron-boe-modelos-daily schedule
Timer files placed in correct systemd directory alongside existing timers
systemd-analyze verify passes on new timer file
Document the gap and fix in AGENTS.md
userStories.verificationCommand: systemd-analyze verify /etc/systemd/system/esdata-boe-modelos-daily.timer && echo PASS || echo FAIL

userStories.title: Audit 51 undocumented workers — classify and document each
acceptanceCriteria: Every .py worker file classified as one of:
TYPE-A: utility/helper — not standalone, no service needed
TYPE-B: domain module — imported by others, no service needed
TYPE-C: standalone worker that SHOULD have a docker-compose service (missing)
TYPE-D: deprecated/dead code
Classification written to docs/worker-inventory.md
Any TYPE-C workers added as new stories in prd.json
userStories.verificationCommand: test -f docs/worker-inventory.md && grep -c 'TYPE-' docs/worker-inventory.md | grep -vE '^0$' && echo PASS || echo FAIL

userStories.title: Test hermes_monitor.py — operational status
acceptanceCriteria: hermes service starts without crash
Monitor produces at least one log line within 30 seconds
If external config required (tokens, endpoints), document in AGENTS.md
If not production-ready, mark clearly as stub in code comments
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose run --rm hermes python hermes_monitor.py 2>&1 | head -20; echo EXIT:$?

userStories.title: Create scripts/integrity-check.sql
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at scripts/integrity-check.sql
Script checks: FK violations, NULL scan on critical columns, duplicate key scan
Script runs cleanly on current DB and returns zero violations
userStories.verificationCommand: test -f scripts/integrity-check.sql && docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -f scripts/integrity-check.sql && echo PASS || echo FAIL

userStories.title: Run DB integrity check — FK, NULLs, duplicates on all populated tables
acceptanceCriteria: Zero FK violations across all tables
Zero unexpected NULLs in non-nullable compliance columns
Zero duplicate rows on logical primary keys
All populated tables have source_url or boe_reference where applicable
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -f scripts/integrity-check.sql 2>&1 | grep -i 'violation\|ERROR\|DUPLICATE' | wc -l | grep '^0$' && echo PASS || echo FAIL-INTEGRITY

userStories.title: Test all API endpoints — health, legislacion, buscar, mcp
acceptanceCriteria: /health returns 200 with valid JSON
/v1/legislacion/LIVA/articulos/1 returns article content (not empty, not 404)
/v1/buscar with a known term returns >= 1 result
/v1/mcp responds and lists tools without error
All responses have Content-Type: application/json
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose up -d api && sleep 3 && curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT:-8000}/health | jq .status && curl -sf 'http://localhost:${API_PORT:-8000}/v1/legislacion/LIVA/articulos/1' | jq '.id // empty' && echo PASS || echo FAIL

userStories.title: MCP tool accuracy spot-check — compliance and legislative queries
acceptanceCriteria: Query for LIVA article 1 returns correct BOE-sourced text, not empty
Query for known BOE identifier returns correct document
Query for repealed provision does NOT return it as current law
Query with absent identifier returns explicit not-found, not fabricated data
All responses include boe_reference or source_url in metadata
userStories.verificationCommand: echo 'Manual spot-check — run test queries via /v1/mcp, compare against boe.es, document in progress.txt. Mark passes:true only when all checks confirmed.'

userStories.title: Verify query_audit_log captures MCP invocations and is append-only
acceptanceCriteria: After calling /v1/mcp, a new row appears in query_audit_log
Row contains: tool_name, input, output_summary, timestamp
UPDATE on query_audit_log fails (append-only enforcement)
Existing 4 rows are real invocations, not test seeds
userStories.verificationCommand: BEFORE=$(docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM query_audit_log'); curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT:-8000}/v1/mcp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"tool":"search","query":"IVA"}'; AFTER=$(docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM query_audit_log'); [ "$AFTER" -gt "$BEFORE" ] && echo PASS || echo FAIL-NO-AUDIT-ENTRY

userStories.title: Run all 14 cron jobs once — verify clean execution and sync_log entries
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 14 cron jobs runs --run-once without unhandled exception
sync_log receives new entry after each cron run
No cron job exits silently — all produce log output
Document any cron requiring unavailable upstream in progress.txt
userStories.verificationCommand: BEFORE=$(docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sync_log'); docker compose --profile cron run --rm cron-boe-daily 2>&1 | tail -5; AFTER=$(docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -tAc 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sync_log'); [ "$AFTER" -gt "$BEFORE" ] && echo PASS || echo FAIL-NO-SYNC-LOG

userStories.title: Final population check — all critical compliance tables have data
acceptanceCriteria: aeat_modelo: >= 25 rows
modelo_articulo: > 0 rows
articulo: > 950 rows (regression vs baseline 969)
norma: > 0 rows
sync_log: updated within last 24h
query_audit_log: functioning
Final row counts per table documented in progress.txt
userStories.verificationCommand: docker compose exec db psql -U $PGUSER -d $PGDATABASE -c "SELECT table_name, n_live_tup AS rows FROM pg_stat_user_tables WHERE n_live_tup > 0 ORDER BY n_live_tup DESC;"
